quote_of_day.text:
>>2948
Okay, received a message from the other office assistant job as well.

>Hi, this is a simple office assistant post with plus 2 pass or graduate. pl excuse.
Two rejections in a matter of an hour.

Now, there's only one job that I applied to left. No reply from them. Feeling broken now.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of casteist anger moved over the board, bringing scattered thunderstorms of abuse. An intense front of hatred for Lingammed developed after a post about Gobbar Falls on Metro.

A heat wave of misogyny and Islamophobia struck in the afternoon, marked by a rash of posts blaming Hindu men for the dress choices of women.

One anon sparked a downpour of criticism with a call to abolish arranged marriages to address a supposed 'Pajeet dysgenic problem'. Meanwhile, a cold snap of anti-Semitic sentiment chilled the board, with one poster calling for anti-Semitism in India and blaming the BJP.

A brief but intense drizzle of criticism followed, with many condemning the post. A separate squall of anger against Modi erupted, with one anon calling for immediate action on a random issue.

articles.title: Gobbar Falls on Metro
articles.body:
Anon started the thread with a two-letter post: "Lingammed".

One anon commented on a video, presumably showing a woman, referred to as "gobbar", falling on a metro, with the remark that in India, laws supposedly discriminate against men, making such incidents inevitable.

Another anon responded, sarcastically asking where were the "strong independent women" when needed. Anon brought up the idea that in India, it's supposedly easy for women to file false rape cases and damage a man's career and reputation.

They also mentioned that acquittals do not necessarily imply innocence. The discussion included a reference to a documentary called "Codex Pajeet 2.0", with one anon praising its narration style.

The tone of the conversation was largely cynical and mocking, with many anons expressing detachment or amusement at the incident. Some joked about not wanting to protect women or get involved in such situations.

One anon even suggested that men should just "mind their own business" in such cases. The incident itself was described as a woman, allegedly strong and independent, being unable to pull herself up from a low platform.

Anons ridiculed her inability to save herself. The thread also included some references to science and history, with one anon sharing links to information about Fritz Haber.

The overall conversation revolved around criticizing and mocking the woman in the video, as well as the broader issues of women's behavior and India's legal system.

articles.title: Anon Suggests Abolishing Arrange Marriages to Address 'Pajeet Dysgenic Problem'
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion by stating that to solve the 'pajeet dysgenic problem,' India needs to abolish arrange marriages first.

Another anon responded, suggesting that abolishing arrange marriages would lead to more poor 'aryanpyaaris' marrying rich 'dasyus,' as the government has economically disadvantaged the aryans. This poster also mentioned that one should 'just slay the dasyus man.

It ain't that hard.' A third anon agreed that arrange marriages need to be abolished and suggested that only successful men should be allowed to pass down their genes, implying that failed individuals like themselves should not be allowed to breed.

The conversation took a eugenic turn, with one anon suggesting that 90% or more of certain groups should be sterilized and their wombs used to rear children of a different group.

Another user countered that arrange marriages are actually eugenic, as they take family background and caste into account.

The thread also involved discussions about the features and potential backgrounds of certain individuals, with some speculating about their caste or ethnicity.

One user questioned the caste of a person in a picture, while another suggested that a person might be of atypical Indian features. The conversation remained divisive, with some users exchanging insults and personal attacks.

One user called another 'abbo' and claimed they had never been outside their own land. The discussion did not appear to reach a conclusive end.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Anti-Semitism in India, Blames BJP
articles.body:
Anon posted a message calling for the annihilation of the BJP and advocating for anti-Semitism in India. The original poster claimed that the BJP has become gynocentric and has sold itself to Jewish people.

Another anon responded by joking about a military pilot being a 'cuck' and suggesting he should kidnap women to create a harem.

A different anon expressed similar sentiments, stating that India is beyond repair and that the BJP's gynocentrism has led to the downfall of Hinduism.

One respondent mentioned that certain laws, such as the Hindu Marriage Act, were enacted to harm Hindu men and predicted a significant decline in the Hindu population by 2050. A brief aside noted this as an unfounded claim.

Another poster simply referred to the discussion as an 'incel thread'. The conversation included several replies with offensive and misogynistic content, including personal attacks and vulgar language. Anon also made derogatory comments about women and relationships.

The discussion thread was marked by aggressive and incendiary rhetoric, with several posters using violent and misogynistic language.

articles.title: Hindu Baaps Blamed for Randi Jeetas' Dress Choices
articles.body:
Anon sparked a discussion by claiming that 'randi jeetas' prefer to dress inappropriately in sarees rather than adopting modern clothing. They blamed 'Hindu baaps' for enabling this behavior.

One respondent argued that the BJP, a right-wing party, prioritizes the wealthy and has not done enough to help poor Hindus, while another claimed the party has 'cucked' and supports 'randipana,' or feminist and woke ideologies.

The conversation turned to income disparities between Hindus and Muslims, with some arguing that the gap has narrowed under BJP rule.

articles.title: Anon slams Modi, sparks discussion on random topics
articles.body: Anon posted a critical comment about Prime Minister Modi, calling him 'nalla modi' and stating he prioritizes 'stupid stuff' over pressing issues. Other users responded with seemingly unrelated topics. One shared YouTube links, describing a party scene with many beer cans. Another mentioned a blog by 'captain Ajit vadakkayil'. The conversation meandered, with some users exchanging brief comments and others expressing interest in the blog or YouTube video.
